articles.title: Virginia’s redistricting referendum shows how the House fight has moved from Congress to state ballots
articles.summary: Voters in Virginia approved a mid-decade redistricting referendum on Tuesday, opening the door for lawmakers to redraw the state’s congressional map and intensifying a national battle over who gets to shape the U.S. House before the 2026 midterms.

articles.title: America’s arts institutions are entering a new season of politics, austerity, and reinvention

articles.title: Why April’s inflation report matters more for household budgets than for Wall Street
articles.summary: March’s CPI showed prices still rising faster than the Fed wants, with energy, shelter, and import-sensitive goods complicating the outlook just as pay gains offer only partial help to families.

articles.title: As federal student aid shifts toward Treasury, colleges and borrowers face a test of capacity
articles.summary: The Trump administration is shifting some student-aid functions toward the Treasury Department while FAFSA support, loan oversight, and federal staffing remain under strain, raising a basic question: is Washington simplifying aid, or merely moving its bottlenecks around?

articles.title: Measles is surging across the Americas, testing the region’s public health defenses
articles.summary: PAHO says the Americas reported 15,366 confirmed measles cases in the first 14 epidemiological weeks of 2026, exposing vaccination gaps, delayed detection and strain on public health systems.

articles.title: Trump’s 2027 science budget revives a familiar fight over the future of U.S. research
articles.summary: The White House wants to shrink NASA, NSF and NIH science accounts again. Congress rejected similar cuts last year, but the new proposal would still force agencies, labs and universities to plan for a smaller federal research state.

articles.title: War in the Middle East is redrawing the region’s human map
articles.summary: A recent reading from the IMF, the World Bank, UNHCR, OCHA, WHO and UNCTAD suggests the conflict is no longer being measured only in military terms. It is pushing people across borders, squeezing trade routes and testing humanitarian systems from Gaza to Lebanon, Chad and the Gulf.
